signals.reasoning: BTC is still structurally strong after the squeeze from 70.7K into the mid-74Ks, but the live board is under a VETO headline tied to the Hormuz escalation and short-squeeze narrative. Trend support is real, yet this is the wrong spot to force a fresh call into geopolitically driven volatility.

ETH is also trend-supported with price and open interest both higher, but it is already extended off the Sunday flush and still directly exposed to the same live geopolitical event brake. Under VETO, the clean read is to watch continuation rather than chase it.

SOL recovered hard with supportive price/OI structure, but that strength is already known and the current setup is being driven by the same event-sensitive squeeze backdrop as BTC. Positive funding plus a live veto headline makes a fresh signal lower quality than simply standing aside.

PNUT is up sharply on the day and already looks like a late-stage squeeze extension rather than an early entry. With thin liquidity, wide execution risk, and a live VETO backdrop, it is better treated as watch-only.

LDO has rebounded well, but the move is already stretched and funding is positive rather than supportive of a fresh squeeze long. Into a live geopolitical veto, that is not a clean enough edge to upgrade.

XPL is another strong bounce name, but it is already well off the lows and still trades with small-cap style execution risk. The event brake matters more than the raw recovery strength here, so the right call is to keep it on watch.
BOME has already run too far to trust as a fresh long and is too noisy to fade cleanly while the market is still reacting to the squeeze headline. That combination makes HOLD the disciplined answer.

MORPHO is one of the cleaner underlying structures on this sheet, but it is still trading inside the same live macro/event regime and does not have enough independent edge to override the VETO. It is interesting underneath, just not actionable right now.

TST is up almost 30% with open interest exploding, which makes it look more like an already-expanded squeeze than a clean new entry. Under a live VETO headline, that profile is too crowded and too unstable to endorse.
KNEIRO lacks robust snapshot and candle context here, and the available read is already distorted by the active event-driven market regime. Thin data plus a VETO board is a straightforward no-trade.
